steps.title: Range of set B
steps.content:
Adding a constant to all data values preserves the range. Since the capacities in set A range from 2 to 14 μF:

$$\text{Range of B} = (14 + 17) - (2 + 17) = 12 \text{ μF}$$

This eliminates choices B and D.

steps.title: Mean of set A from the dot plot
steps.content:
The capacitors in set A follow a symmetric distribution centered at 8 μF. The columns at values 5 and 11 μF have the same height, columns at 2 and 14 μF have equal height, and the tallest column is at 8 μF (the center). By symmetry:

$$\text{Mean of A} = 8 \text{ μF}$$

steps.title: Mean of set B
steps.content:
When each capacity increases by 17 μF, the mean also increases by 17 μF:

$$\text{Mean of B} = 8 + 17 = 25 \text{ μF}$$

With mean = 25 μF and range = 12 μF, that matches choice C — C is correct.
